data.summary:
Read https://www.cnbc.com/2025/03/12/intel-appoints-lip-bu-tan-as-new-ceo-stock-up-11percent.html:
Intel announced on Wednesday, March 12, 2025, that Lip-Bu Tan was appointed as new CEO. Tan, previously CEO of Cadence Design Systems and a former Intel board member, replaces interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and MJ Holthaus who took over in December 2024 when Pat Gelsinger was ousted. Zinsner returns to CFO role and Holthaus remains in charge of Intel Products. No specific effective date beyond the announcement is provided in the article.
Key facts:
  - Announcement date: Wednesday, March 12, 2025
  - Lip-Bu Tan appointed as Intel CEO
  - Replaces interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and MJ Holthaus
  - Interim co-CEOs took over in December 2024 when Pat Gelsinger was ousted
  - Zinsner returns to CFO; Holthaus remains as Intel Products lead
  - Tan was previously Cadence Design Systems CEO and departed Intel board last year

data.summary:
Read https://www.neowin.net/news/intel-appoints-lip-bu-tan-as-its-new-ceo/:
Lip-Bu Tan was appointed as Intel's new CEO effective March 18, 2025. He formally succeeded interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus, who had taken over after Pat Gelsinger's retirement in early December 2024. Tan is a veteran of the semiconductor industry with over 20 years of experience and previously served as CEO of Cadence Design Systems from 2009 to 2021.
Key facts:
  - Lip-Bu Tan appointed as Intel CEO effective March 18, 2025
  - He succeeded interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus
  - Previous CEO Pat Gelsinger retired in early December 2024 after four years leading Intel
  - Tan was CEO of Cadence Design Systems from 2009 to 2021
  - Tan joined Intel's board of directors in 2021
  - Tan holds degrees in physics, nuclear engineering, and an MBA; received the 2022 Robert N. Noyce Award

data.answer:
## Intel's 2025 Permanent CEO Appointment

**Lip-Bu Tan** was appointed as Intel's permanent CEO in 2025 [1][2].

| Detail | Information |
|---|---|
| **Person appointed** | Lip-Bu Tan |
| **Announcement date** | Wednesday, **March 12, 2025** |
| **Effective date** | **March 18, 2025** |
| **Immediate predecessors (succeeded)** | Interim co-CEOs **David Zinsner** and **Michelle Johnston Holthaus** |
| **Original predecessor** | **Pat Gelsinger**, who was ousted/retired in early December 2024 |

### Key Context
- After Pat Gelsinger departed Intel in **December 2024**, the company was led temporarily by **two interim co-CEOs**: CFO **David Zinsner** and Intel Products CEO **Michelle Johnston Holthaus** [1][2].
- Lip-Bu Tan formally replaced this interim co-CEO arrangement when he officially took the helm on **March 18, 2025** [2].
- Upon Tan's appointment, Zinsner returned to his CFO role and Holthaus continued leading Intel Products [1].
- Tan is a semiconductor industry veteran who previously served as CEO of **Cadence Design Systems** (2009–2021) and had also served on Intel's board of directors [1][2].

citations.title: Intel appoints Lip-Bu Tan as CEO to orchestrate turnaround at struggling chipmaker – CNBC
citations.note: Confirms announcement date of March 12, 2025, Tan's appointment, and that he succeeded interim co-CEOs Zinsner and Holthaus after Gelsinger's ouster in December 2024.

citations.title: Intel appoints Lip-Bu Tan as its new CEO – Neowin
citations.note: Confirms effective date of March 18, 2025, full names of interim co-CEOs succeeded, and Gelsinger's departure in early December 2024.

data.confidence: high — All four key facts (person, announcement date, effective date, predecessor(s)) are confirmed by two independent, credible sources (CNBC and Neowin) that were fully read during this research session.
data.open_gaps: None significant. The precise reason for Gelsinger's exit (described variously as "ousted," "retired," or mutually agreed departure) has some nuance, but all sources agree on the December 2024 timeline and the resulting interim co-CEO arrangement.
